# ARC Report

> ARC Report tracks 1,000+ e-commerce brands daily, scanning each site for AI agent readiness — robots.txt policies, structured data, platform detection, and access controls. It provides a public, searchable index of how the retail web treats autonomous AI agents.

## About

ARC Report (arcreport.ai) is a research tool that monitors the AI-agent readiness of major e-commerce brands. Every day it crawls brand storefronts and evaluates whether AI agents like GPTBot, ClaudeBot, and others are permitted or blocked, whether structured data is present, which e-commerce platform powers the site, and how the checkout flow behaves for non-human visitors.

## API

- [API Documentation](/docs): Full reference for the ARC Report API.
- [Brand Matrix](/api/matrix): Raw JSON matrix of all tracked brands and their current readiness signals.
- [Changelog](/api/changelog): Feed of recent changes detected across tracked brands.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://arcreport.ai/): Live dashboard and search across all tracked brands.
- [Brand Pages](https://arcreport.ai/brand/): Per-brand detail pages with historical data and screenshots.
- [API Docs](https://arcreport.ai/docs): Developer documentation for integrating ARC Report data.

## Data

ARC Report collects the following signals for each brand:

- **robots.txt policies**: Which AI user-agents are allowed or blocked.
- **Structured data**: Presence of Schema.org Product, Offer, and BreadcrumbList markup.
- **Platform detection**: Identified e-commerce platform (Shopify, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Magento, custom, etc.).
- **Checkout accessibility**: Whether the add-to-cart and checkout flows are navigable by an agent.
- **Cookie and consent gates**: Whether cookie banners or consent walls block automated access.

Data is refreshed daily. The full dataset is available via the API.
